content:
- Completed story-21 task 03 and marked the task file `<status>completed</status> <passes>true</passes>` with acceptance criteria checked and execution outcome recorded; updated the attached plan with completed TDD slices and final verification evidence.
- Enforced the publish gate through explicit `validate-fast` and `validate-long` jobs in `.github/workflows/publish-images.yml`, updated the workflow contract/README boundaries, and fixed real repo test-harness issues that surfaced while proving the task (Postgres port-ownership race plus long-lane harness boundary assumptions).
- Final required gates all passed after the last edits: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`.
- Ran `/bin/bash .ralph/task_switch.sh`, committed all changes as `5fcfee6` with the required task-finished message, and pushed `main` to origin.
- Next if resuming unexpectedly: no task-03 work remains; start from the new current Ralph task pointer instead of reopening this task.

- Loaded the list-active-tasks skill and read previous progress for Choose-Task.
- Starting task selection by refreshing Ralph task summary and reading active/done chooser files.
- Next: resolve blockers, apply bug-first and priority rules, write chosen task path to .ralph/current_task.txt, then stop.

- Refreshed Ralph summaries and read current_tasks plus current_tasks_done.
- Observed no active bug entries in current_tasks so selection likely comes from unblocked failing non-bug work.
- Next: verify priority tags and blocker completion, then write the chosen task path and stop.

- Confirmed story-21 blocker task 02b is already done in current_tasks_done, which unblocks story-21 tasks 01 and 08.
- Reading repo shape and key unblocked task files to choose the most logical next task based on actual stack and story sequencing.
- Next: decide between the highest-leverage unblocked failing tasks, write the selected path to .ralph/current_task.txt, then stop.

- Compared unblocked verify-service tasks with newly unblocked GitHub workflow story work.
- Story 21 looks like the current delivery bottleneck because later novice-user verification depends on published registry artifacts and the repo has a real publish-images workflow present.
- Next: confirm story-21 ordering against existing files, then set .ralph/current_task.txt to the selected task path and stop.

- Selected the next task and wrote it to .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Chosen task: .ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/01-task-fix-github-workflows-to-build-test-and-publish-the-three-image-split.md
- Why: no unblocked failing bugs exist, story-21 blocker 02b already passes, and task 01 best matches the actual codebase/workflow state as the broad unblocked publication bottleneck before downstream novice-user verification.

- Read prior progress log, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` / `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the current task is story-21 task 01 (`.ralph/tasks/story-21-github-workflows-image-publish/01-task-fix-github-workflows-to-build-test-and-publish-the-three-image-split.md`).
- Next: inspect the task file status and either create/update a plan with `TO BE VERIFIED` or execute the approved plan.

- Read the task file and found an existing plan path for story-21 task 01.
- The task itself is still marked `not_started` / `<passes>false</passes>` and references blocker task `02b`.
- Next: inspect the attached plan status and blocker task state to decide whether to execute now or return the plan to `TO BE VERIFIED`.

- Confirmed the attached plan ends in `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn must execute rather than re-plan.
- Verified blocker task `02b` is completed with `<passes>true</passes>`.
- Next: inspect the current workflow/test boundary and worktree state, then run the required repo gates for task-01 and only make code/task-file edits if gaps remain.

- Entered execution mode for story-21 task 01 because the attached plan ends with NOW EXECUTE.
- Inspected the publish workflow, workflow-contract tests, published-image boundary, and Git remotes; current code appears to already match the planned end state.
- Next: run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`, then use `/home/joshazimullah.linux/github-api-curl` to inspect a real hosted `publish-images` run and only then update the task state.

- Required local gates are green for task-01: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed on the current `main` worktree.
- Next: use the authenticated GitHub API wrapper to inspect a real `publish-images` workflow run, including job status and log evidence, before deciding whether task-01 can be marked complete.

- Hosted inspection of the latest completed successful run showed real publish success, but it was still the older single-`validate` workflow shape.
- Next: inspect the current head run on `main` to confirm the hosted workflow now matches the split `validate-fast` / `validate-long` definition that is present locally before I update task completion state.

- Hosted run `24643444655` for head `5fcfee60d11ca39e52830c5b6d9707114882b5f0` completed successfully on `main`.
- Next: download the run logs, verify the masked derived-credential line is redacted in the published logs, and then update the task markdown with the exact evidence before task switching and commit/push.

- Updated story-21 task 01 to `<status>completed</status> <passes>true</passes>` and recorded exact local and hosted evidence, including run `24643444655` plus masked-log confirmation.
- Next: run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, review the resulting Ralph file changes, commit all required files, and push `main`.
